finding: §4.1 (and §12.2) say that `tp resume <spec>` whose adjacent task file does not yet exist computes the phase 'from review state alone (§4.3, rule 1)'. But rule 1 only yields `review` when review is unconverged/absent; if that spec's review sequence is genuinely converged and no task file exists, rule 1 does not fire, and rule 2 requires an existing zero-task init-shell task file to select `decompose`. No rule matches a converged-review + absent-task-file state, leaving the phase undefined.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Either define that an absent task file is treated as the zero-task init shell for rule 2 (so converged review + no task file → decompose), or add an explicit rule mapping 'no task file, review converged' to `decompose`. Remove the claim that rule 1 alone resolves the absent-task-file case.

finding: For a glob keep-entry, `kept[].path` is ambiguous. §4.2 item 4 says `kept` is "the keep-list entries that currently match" (implying `path` is the glob pattern from the entry, one row per entry), while §4.5 says "a path matched by a keep_uncommitted entry ... goes to kept" (implying `path` is the concrete matched working-tree file, one row per matched file). With a glob entry matching three files, these produce different `kept` arrays. §12 never uses a glob, so the shape is untestable as written.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Pin whether `kept[].path` echoes the keep-entry pattern (one row per entry) or the concrete matched path (one row per file), and whether `kept` may contain multiple rows for one glob entry; add a §12 assertion for a glob keep-entry matching multiple files.

finding: Rule 1 keys phase on `tp review --status` reporting `converged: false`, but tp's existing `engine.Converged()` returns false whenever the spec is stale (`Converged = ConsecutiveClean >= required && !StateStale`; reviewstate.go:197). So a review that met its clean-round threshold but whose spec later changed reports `converged: false`, which makes rule 1 fire and set phase=`review`. This directly contradicts the §4.3 paragraph 'When the review sequence is converged but the spec has since changed (stale flag set), phase stays decompose/implement ... surfaced as a blocker rather than reverting the phase.' The precondition 'converged but stale' is unreachable via the `converged` field the rule reads, and the rule reverts exactly the phase the paragraph forbids.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Pin rule 1 to the stale-independent predicate (clean-round threshold reached, i.e. ConsecutiveClean >= required_clean_rounds) rather than the composite `converged`, and handle staleness only through blocker item 4. State explicitly that phase detection ignores the stale bit and that stale never reverts phase to review.

finding: The L75 fix did not resolve the contradiction. §4.4.3 and §12.3 still tie the implement payload to `tp next --peek --minimal` while also requiring it to surface the WIP task ('the WIP task if one exists (wip: true)'; §12.3: 'payload.task equals what tp next --peek --minimal reports (WIP task with wip: true if any...)'). But `tp next --peek` ignores WIP and always returns the first ready task — the exact behavior L75 established. An implementer wiring the payload to `tp next --peek --minimal` gets first-ready, never the WIP task, contradicting the same sentence; §12.3 now bakes the false claim into an acceptance criterion.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Stop sourcing the payload from `tp next --peek` (which ignores WIP). Specify tp resume's own selection independently ('the WIP task if one exists, else the first ready task — the resume-or-claim order, not --peek'), or, if v0.28.0 makes `tp next --peek` WIP-aware, state that behavior change explicitly (and not as a Non-Goal).

finding: §4.5 and §7.1 say a keep_uncommitted `path` may be a `glob` and that `tp resume` matches an uncommitted path against it, but no glob dialect is defined. It is unspecified whether `*` crosses directory separators (does `src/*` match `src/a/b.go`?), whether `**` is supported, whether matching is Go filepath.Match, doublestar, or shell semantics, and whether leading `./` or trailing `/` are normalized. Two engineers would write contradictory tests. §12.4/§12.5 only exercise the exact-path case (`a.txt`), never a glob, so the semantics are never pinned.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: State the exact glob dialect (e.g. Go doublestar with `*` not crossing `/` and `**` crossing it), how paths are normalized before matching, and add a §12 case asserting a concrete glob entry (e.g. `logs/*.tmp`) matching and not matching specific files.

finding: Blocker items 3 (`review budget exhausted`/`audit budget exhausted`) and 4 (`spec changed since review converged`) have NO acceptance test in §12 — §12.3/§12.4 only cover items 1 and 2. Item 4 depends on a `stale` flag (§4.3, §4.6 item 4) whose origin, storage, and how a test induces it are never defined in this spec, so no deterministic pass/fail test can be written for it. Item 3's trigger (recorded round count reaching review_max_rounds/audit_max_rounds without convergence) is also never asserted.

suggestion: Add §12 cases for both blockers: pin how the `stale`/spec-changed state is produced and detected, and construct a budget-exhausted fixture (rounds == max_rounds, unconverged) asserting the exact blocker string.

finding: The L89 fix (surface staleness as a §4.6 item-4 blocker instead of reverting the phase) reintroduces the L77 driver deadlock the keep-list eliminated for dirty trees. §9.2's reference driver runs `next_action` only when `blockers` is empty and otherwise must 'clear them,' but defines a clearing action only for `unexplained changes` (commit/keep) and budget-exhausted (raise cap). The `spec changed since review converged` blocker (item 4 — realistic: editing a spec mid-implementation, where phase stays `implement`) and `no ready task` (item 2) have no defined resolution, so the driver can neither clear them nor proceed — the same non-empty-blockers deadlock L77 named.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Classify blockers as advisory vs must-clear: staleness and no-ready-task are surfaced but must NOT halt `next_action`; only `unexplained changes` and budget-exhausted gate the loop. Update §4.6/§9.2 so the driver runs `next_action` when only advisory blockers are present, or define an explicit clearing action for every blocker.

finding: The reference-driver pseudocode lists "raise a cap" among the blockers the loop clears autonomously ("if blockers is non-empty, clear them (reconcile unexplained changes, raise a cap, and so on)"). This contradicts §4.6 item 3 and the standing tp invariant (CLAUDE.md self-development rule) that raising review_max_rounds/audit_max_rounds is a user-approved decision, "never the agent's own." As written, the driver auto-raises caps on `review/audit budget exhausted`, defeating the budget gate.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Split blocker handling in §9.2: the driver auto-clears only agent-safe blockers (unexplained changes -> commit or `tp keep`); `review/audit budget exhausted` and any cap change must escalate to the human, matching §4.6 item 3. Reword to "clear agent-resolvable blockers; escalate budget-exhausted/stale blockers to the operator."

finding: For a glob keep-list entry (e.g. `*.log`) that matches several uncommitted files, it is ambiguous what `kept` contains. §4.2 item 4 calls kept 'the keep-list entries ... echoed' (one entry per glob, path = the glob pattern), while §4.5 says 'a path matched ... goes to kept with its reason' (one entry per concrete matched file, path = the real file). These yield different arrays, and if two globs match the same file it is also unspecified which reason wins.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Pin it: kept holds one object per concrete uncommitted path that matched, with `path` = the real file path and `reason` = the matching entry's reason; define first-match-wins (list order) when multiple entries match one path.

finding: The ordering of `changes` and `kept` is never pinned. §4.2 items 3-4 call them "an array of ..." and §4.5 derives them from `git status --porcelain`, but it is unstated whether the arrays follow git's emission order or are sorted lexicographically. Two engineers writing exact-array assertions (§12.4 lists a single path, avoiding the question) would disagree on the expected order for multiple changed paths, making multi-path tests non-deterministic.

suggestion: Specify a deterministic order (e.g. sort `changes` and `kept` lexicographically by `path`) and assert it in §12.4 with at least two changed paths.

finding: Phase rule 4 ("all tasks done and the audit sequence is not converged → audit") lacks the explicit "or no audit state exists yet" clause that rule 1 spells out for review ("...or no review state exists yet → review"). For a project that finished implementation but has never run audit, it is unstated whether an absent audit sequence counts as "not converged" (→ audit) — there is no fallthrough rule for all-done + no-audit-state, since rule 5 requires audit converged. The asymmetry with rule 1 invites contradictory tests.

suggestion: Add the parity clause to rule 4 ("...audit sequence is not converged, or no audit state exists yet → audit") and assert the never-audited case in §12.1.

finding: The revision pinned the review side of phase detection (rule 1: `tp review --status reports converged: false`, plus an explicit 'or no review state exists yet → review' clause) but left the audit side L90 flagged unaddressed. Rules 4/5 say only 'the audit sequence is not converged/converged' — no command/flag names the audit convergence source, and there is no analog of the no-state clause, so whether a zero-round audit (all tasks done, no audit yet) is 'not converged → audit' or vacuously 'converged → release' remains undefined. §12.1 asserts the outcome but pins no source, so the audit half of the fix is incomplete.

suggestion: Mirror rule 1 for audit: name the convergence source (e.g. `tp audit --status reports converged: false`) and add 'or no audit state exists yet → audit', so a fresh all-tasks-done project deterministically enters `audit`.

finding: §4.1/§12.2 assert that a `tp resume <spec>` whose adjacent task file is absent computes phase "from review state alone (§4.3, rule 1)," i.e. review. But rule 1 only yields `review` when the review sequence is unconverged or absent; rules 2-5 all read the task file. If the spec's review sequence is already converged while the adjacent task file is absent (e.g. deleted after review), rule 1's condition is false and rules 2-5 have no task file to evaluate, so first-match-wins matches nothing — the phase is undefined, contradicting the "(rule 1)" claim.

suggestion: Add a default/fallthrough to §4.3 (e.g. "absent task file => treat as zero tasks" so a converged review yields `decompose`, or explicitly exit 3), and correct §4.1/§12.2 to reference the actual resolved phase rather than asserting rule 1 unconditionally.

finding: The `review`/`audit` payload `{round, unresolved_findings}` (§4.4 items 1,4) does not define the integers. `round` could be the count of completed rounds (`len(rounds)`) or the next round number (`len(rounds)+1`); `unresolved_findings` could be the count from the latest recorded round only or the running total across all rounds still unresolved after `--resolve`. Each yields a different displayed number.

suggestion: Pin `round` to a single meaning (e.g. the 1-based number of the round the emitted prompts belong to = last recorded + 1) and define `unresolved_findings` as the count of recorded findings not yet marked fixed/wontfix/duplicate across the sequence.

finding: The decompose payload is `{"spec": "<path>"}` (§4.4 item 2), but that path is already emitted as the top-level `spec` field (§4.2 item 2) in the same JSON object, and decompose's `next_action.command` is null. The payload therefore re-emits a string the agent already parsed and adds no field it does not already hold — a duplicated path on every resume in the decompose phase.

suggestion: Make the decompose payload `{}` (mirroring the release payload, §4.4 item 5); the agent reads the spec from the top-level `spec` field. Removes a fully duplicated path string with zero information loss.

finding: The exact literal of `next_action.command` is not pinned even though §4.2 calls it "the literal next tp command as a string." §4.4 shows `tp review <spec>`/`tp audit <spec>` and `tp next` in placeholder form; it is unstated whether the spec path is substituted (absolute vs repo-relative), whether `--file` is appended when discovery was used, and why `implement` embeds no task id while review/audit embed the spec. §12.3 keeps the `<spec>` placeholder, so an exact-match assertion is impossible.

finding: The integer payload values for `review`/`audit` are not pinned. `round` (§4.4 items 1,4) is undefined as to whether it is the next round to run or the last recorded round, and whether it is 0- or 1-indexed for a spec with no rounds yet. `unresolved_findings` has no definition of "unresolved" (recorded-minus-resolved? per-round or cumulative? across all roles?). §12.3 says only that `payload` "carries round and unresolved_findings" without asserting a value, so two engineers derive different expected numbers.

finding: Keep-list matching is described as 'exact path or glob' (§4.5, §7.3) but the glob dialect is never pinned: whether `*` crosses `/` (Go `filepath.Match` vs doublestar `**`), whether `**` is supported for recursive directory match, case sensitivity, and how a trailing-slash directory pattern behaves. Two implementations would classify the same tree differently.

suggestion: Name the exact matcher (e.g. Go `filepath.Match` semantics where `*` does not cross `/`, or explicitly support `**`), state case sensitivity, and give one worked example (does `logs/*` match `logs/a/b.txt`).

finding: §4.5 enumerates only "tracked modifications, staged changes, and non-ignored untracked files," leaving other `git status --porcelain` states unpinned: deletions (` D`), renames (`R  old -> new` — which path is reported, and is `old` counted?), copies (`C`), unmerged/conflicted (`UU`), and the both-staged-and-worktree-dirty case (`MM`, where a path could be listed twice unless dedup is specified). No §12 case covers any of these, so tests over a realistic dirty tree are non-deterministic.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Enumerate the full porcelain XY handling (which codes count, how rename paths are extracted, that each path appears at most once) and add a §12 case with a deletion and a rename.

finding: §4.5 says to read `git status --porcelain` and derive paths, but does not specify parsing of the format's edge cases: renames/copies emit `R  orig -> dest` / `C  orig -> dest` (the path field holds two paths and an arrow), and paths containing spaces or non-ASCII are C-quoted (`"path with space"`) unless `-z` is used. A naive 'strip the status prefix, take the rest as path' parser mishandles all of these, corrupting the `changes`/`kept` partition.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Specify parsing precisely — prefer `git status --porcelain -z` (NUL-separated, no quoting) and define, for a rename, which side (dest, or both) becomes the tracked path used for keep-list matching.

finding: §4.5 states 'non-ignored untracked files each count as an uncommitted change', implying per-file enumeration, but `git status --porcelain` by default collapses an untracked directory into a single `?? dir/` entry rather than listing each file. Without `--untracked-files=all` (`-uall`), a keep-list entry like `dir/*.tmp` cannot match the collapsed `dir/` path, so intentional untracked files inside a new directory would surface as unexplained `changes`.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Require `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` so each untracked file is listed individually, and state that explicitly in §4.5.

finding: Two blocker-ordering questions are unpinned. (a) `blockers` is "an array of strings" (§4.2 item 6) that can hold several conditions at once (e.g. `unexplained changes` plus `review budget exhausted`), but the order of entries in the array is never specified. (b) Within blocker item 2, `no ready task: blocked by <id>[, <id>...]` does not pin the order of the id list (insertion order? sorted? topological?). §12.3 asserts the blocker "names the unmet dependencies" without pinning order, so exact-string tests are non-deterministic.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Pin a deterministic order for the `blockers` array (e.g. fixed vocabulary order) and for the id list inside `no ready task` (e.g. sorted ascending); assert both with a multi-blocker / multi-dep fixture in §12.

finding: §4.2/§4.6 allow `blockers` to be a set (array) of multiple simultaneous conditions, but no §12 case exercises coexistence. It is untested/unstated whether `unexplained changes` can appear together with the phase-specific blocker (budget exhausted, spec-changed, no-ready-task), and whether `next_action` is withheld while ANY blocker is present. Every §12 scenario produces at most one blocker.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Add a §12 case with two blockers present at once (e.g. unexplained changes + no ready task) asserting both strings appear and next_action is still emitted but gated.

finding: §4.6 items 1-3 each name a concrete clearing action (commit/`tp keep`; wait on deps; raise cap), but item 4 `spec changed since review converged` names none, and §4.3 explicitly keeps the phase at decompose/implement where next_action.command is `null`/`tp next` — neither of which re-runs review. The reference driver (§9.2) is told to "clear" blockers, but there is no defined or machine-readable path to clear the stale blocker, breaking the otherwise-uniform blocker contract.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Define the stale-blocker remedy (e.g. re-run `tp review <spec>` until re-converged, which clears the stale flag) and either encode it in next_action for the stale case or state in §4.6 item 4 that it is a human/agent escalation like budget exhaustion.

finding: `tp config --resolved` reports commit_strategy as {value:<resolved>, source, effective}, and §5.1 says commit_strategy "resolves to one of builtin/hc/auto." For an unknown authored value (e.g. `squash`) that "resolves to builtin," it is unspecified whether reported `value` echoes the raw token `squash` (which violates the {auto,builtin,hc} domain) or the fallback `builtin` (which duplicates `effective` and hides the offending token from the JSON, leaving it only in the stderr warning). The auto case keeps value="auto" and pushes detection into `effective`, so by analogy the unknown->builtin mapping is ambiguous.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: State explicitly that `value` echoes the raw authored token (including unknowns) and `effective` is the concrete builtin|hc after both auto-detection and the unknown->builtin fallback; and relax §5.1's "resolves to one of the three" to "the effective strategy is one of builtin|hc."

finding: For the same command under a resolved `hc` strategy, `tp commit`/`tp done --auto-commit` exit 2 when hc is present (§5.4.2) but exit 4 with a hint to "install hc or set builtin" when hc is absent (§5.4.3). Installing hc — the exit-4 remedy — does not make `tp commit` work: it moves the caller to §5.4.2 where `tp commit` is still rejected (exit 2). The exit-4 diagnosis ("strategy requires hc") is misleading for `tp commit`, since `tp commit` is invalid under hc regardless of hc's presence.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Treat `tp commit`/`--auto-commit` as a usage error (exit 2, "not valid under hc: commit with hc then tp done --commit") whether or not hc is installed, and reserve exit 4 ("hc required but absent") only for paths that actually attempt to make a commit with no fallback. At minimum drop "install hc" from the exit-4 hint for `tp commit`.

finding: Path 3 (explicit `commit_strategy: hc`, hc absent) specifies exit codes only for `tp commit`/`--auto-commit` (exit 4) and for `--commit <sha>`/`--covered-by` (succeed). It says nothing about a bare `tp done <id> "reason"` (no --commit, no --covered-by). Path 2 rejects bare done with exit 2, but under hc-absent it is unspecified whether bare done is exit 2 (usage: strategy needs a commit reference) or exit 4 (state: hc missing). A bare done makes no commit, so the two branches disagree and the implementer must guess.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: State the bare-`tp done` outcome under Path 3: since it makes no commit, reject it with exit 2 and the same hc hint as Path 2 (the missing-hc exit 4 applies only to paths that must *make* a commit).

finding: By §6.1/§6.2 every committed task carries BOTH `commit_sha` and `commit_shas`, and in the common single-commit case `commit_sha == commit_shas[0]` by invariant — two JSON keys for one value on every task. §6.1 justifies retaining `commit_sha` for back-compat readers but never says `--compact` (or task output generally) collapses the redundancy, so both keys are emitted to the agent on every `show`/`plan`/`list`/`report`, violating P3 on the by-far most common path (multi-commit is rare, only the hc flow).

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Specify that `commit_shas` is populated only when it holds >1 element (single-commit tasks carry `commit_sha` alone), or that `--compact` strips `commit_sha` since it is derivable as `commit_shas[0]`. Back-compat readers keep `commit_sha`; agents stop paying for the duplicate key on every committed task.

finding: `keep_uncommitted[].path` is 'repo-relative' (§7.1) while `changes` come from `git status --porcelain` output, whose paths are relative to the invocation directory, not necessarily the repo/.git root. The spec never says `tp resume` normalizes porcelain paths to the same base (the `.git`/`.tp` root) before matching, so running `tp resume` from a subdirectory would compare mismatched bases and misclassify every path.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: State that `tp resume` runs git status at (or resolves paths relative to) the repo root that anchors `.tp/`, and that both keep-list entries and porcelain paths are normalized to that root before matching.

finding: §7.2 defines `tp keep <path> "<reason>"`, `--remove`, and `--list` but no validation/error paths: exit code for a missing `<reason>` argument, whether an empty reason is allowed, the result of `tp keep --remove <path>` on a path not in the list (error vs silent no-op, and which exit code), and whether an absolute or `../`-escaping path is normalized or rejected. An implementer must invent all of these.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Specify: missing reason → exit 2 (usage); reject or normalize non-repo-relative/empty paths; `--remove` of an absent entry is a no-op exit 0 (or exit 4 with a hint) — pick one; state whether an empty reason is permitted.

finding: §7.4 tells the hc flow to feed `tp keep --list` paths into hc's `allow_unplanned`, but `tp resume` already emits `kept` (§4.2 item 4) — the keep entries that currently match an uncommitted change, which is exactly the set `allow_unplanned` needs (a keep entry with no current diff has nothing to allow). The agent is already in the unit context holding the resume output, so mandating `tp keep --list` is an extra round-trip for data already in hand — the precise cost tp exists to eliminate.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Point §7.4 at the `kept[].path` values already returned by `tp resume` (available even under `--compact`, which strips only `reason`); reserve `tp keep --list` for out-of-loop inspection. Saves one round-trip per hc commit.

finding: §3.1 defines a safe checkpoint as "the finished unit's own work is committed and recorded — a task closed with its commit(s)," but the retained builtin path (§5.4.1) still allows a bare `tp done <id> "reason"` to close a task with no commit (only the hc strategy rejects it, §5.4.2). A builtin project can thus produce a `done` task with empty commit_shas and its work still in the working tree — a done-but-uncommitted checkpoint the invariant treats as unreachable; and because the task is already closed, the later task<->commit "recorded" link cannot be added.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Either note in §3.1 that under builtin the commit is advisory (the tree warning of §8.3 is the only enforcement) so a done task may legitimately carry no commit, or align builtin close with hc by warning/erroring on a commitless `tp done` when changes remain.

finding: L104's decompose-payload redundancy is only half-fixed: release was reduced to `{}`, but the decompose payload is still `{"spec": "<path>"}`, which duplicates the top-level `spec` field (§4.2, field 2) present in every phase. The payload carries no information the object does not already hold, so it only adds bytes — the P3 concern L104 raised, now inconsistent with the sibling `{}` release payload.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Make the decompose payload `{}` like release (phase:decompose plus the top-level `spec` already conveys everything), or drop the redundant `spec` key.

finding: The `unexplained changes: <n> path(s)` blocker (§4.6 item 1) interpolates `<n>`, which equals `len(changes)` — and the full `changes` array is already emitted (§4.2 item 3). The agent must string-parse the count when it could read it from the array, and the interpolation makes the declared 'fixed set of machine-readable strings' non-fixed, forcing a prefix/pattern match instead of an equality check.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Emit the blocker as the bare fixed token `unexplained changes`; the agent takes both count and paths from `changes`. Keeps the vocabulary literally fixed (equality-checkable) and drops the duplicated count.

finding: The blocker literal `unexplained changes: <n> path(s)` (§4.6 item 1) does not pin whether `path(s)` is rendered verbatim or pluralized by count (`1 path` vs `3 paths`). §12.4 asserts the blocker is "raised" without pinning its exact string. An exact-match test cannot be written deterministically.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: State the exact rendering — either keep the literal `path(s)` always, or pin singular/plural by n — and assert the exact string in §12.4.

finding: `blockers` is an array and multiple items can co-occur (e.g. `unexplained changes` together with `no ready task` in the `implement` phase, or with the `spec changed` staleness blocker). The spec does not fix the array ordering, leaving output non-deterministic and the reference driver's 'clear them' loop order unspecified.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Define a deterministic order for blockers (e.g. the fixed §4.6 item order 1→4) so the array is stable across runs and testable.

finding: For an unknown `commit_strategy` value (e.g. `squash`), §5.2 says it "resolves to builtin" and warns, but does not pin what `tp config --resolved` shows: whether `value` echoes the raw `squash` or is normalized to `builtin`, and whether `effective` is `builtin`. §12.7 asserts only "resolves to builtin ... prints a stderr warning and exits 0," not the reported `{value, source, effective}` shape for the unknown case.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Pin the reported object for an unknown value (e.g. `value: "squash"`, `effective: "builtin"`, source = the layer it came from) and assert it in §12.7.

finding: The v0.24.0 contract is that `tp config --resolved` annotates each setting uniformly with {value, source}. §5.2 gives commit_strategy a third key `effective`, making it the only setting with a non-uniform object shape, so a consumer iterating `--resolved` output over settings hits one heterogeneous entry.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Either document that `effective` is a setting-specific extension present only where auto-resolution applies, or generalize the `--resolved` per-setting object to always allow an optional `effective` (equal to `value` for non-auto settings) so the shape stays uniform.

finding: L100 asked for a --compact rule for the commit_sha/commit_shas pair; the fix justified the redundancy for backward compatibility (§6.1) but added no compaction. §6.2 makes single-commit the only builtin case and the overwhelmingly common one, so tp emits the identical SHA twice (commit_sha and commit_shas[0]) on nearly every task in `tp plan`/`show`/`list`, and the CLAUDE.md --compact strip set covers neither — the standing P3 cost the finding named is acknowledged but not mitigated.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: Add a --compact rule that omits `commit_shas` when it equals `[commit_sha]` (single-commit tasks) and keeps the full list only for multi-commit tasks; or strip `commit_sha` under --compact and keep only `commit_shas`.

finding: §6.1 pins the invariant that `commit_sha == commit_shas[0]`, but only describes the write path. Pre-0.28.0 done tasks already carry `commit_sha` with no `commit_shas` (new, `omitempty`). The spec does not say whether tp backfills `commit_shas` from `commit_sha` on load/migration, so for legacy tasks the invariant is violated (commit_shas empty, commit_sha set) and new `commit_shas` readers see nothing for historically-committed tasks.

resolved.evidence: Round-2 revision: blockers now {code,message,class} with agent-clearable vs escalate (resolves deadlock/driver-autonomy/stale); phase detection reordered task-first (decouples from staleness); commit-strategy exit-4 removed (tp never invokes hc so hc-absence never fails a tp command; tp commit under hc always exit 2); config --resolved uniform {value,source} + effective in plain config; commit_shas canonical + commit_sha BC mirror documented; glob=filepath.Match pinned, porcelain -z -uall repo-relative parsing pinned, kept reports matched files; payload round/decompose/command literals pinned + deterministic sort; tp keep validation; §7.4 uses resume kept (no extra round-trip).

suggestion: State the read/migration rule: when a task has `commit_sha` but empty `commit_shas`, treat `commit_shas` as `[commit_sha]` (backfill on load or on next write), preserving the invariant for legacy tasks.
